purpose: The discriminator for WHERE a number threshold counts: two disconnected Friends pairs (A-B, C-D) with popular(B) and popular(D) and greater_equal number/total 2 on the popular(y) clause. Each head's own neighborhood holds exactly ONE qualifying grounding (1 < 2), yet trendy(A) and trendy(C) both derive because the threshold consumes the clause's whole-graph qualifying count (2 >= 2) — the pinned gate sits in _ground_rule's per-clause satisfaction check over the clause grounding (interpretation.py:1364-1377), before head candidates are enumerated. The -two/-default twins alone cannot tell clause-level from per-head gating (their lone head also saw the lone qualifier); this case can.
provenance: docs/surface.md type:Threshold happy-number-total (consumption side, clause-vs-head discrimination); interpretation.py:1364-1377/1475-1503 at the pin; screened live 2026-07-07 (review screen_clause_gate.py)
